cognitive_layer.purpose: Scaling transforms genes to have zero mean and unit variance, preventing highly expressed genes from dominating PCA and other analyses. This ensures all genes contribute equally to distance calculations. Max value clipping prevents outliers from distorting the distribution.

parameter_impact.max_value: Truncates values after scaling. 10 is common (about 10x standard deviations). Lower values compress the range more; higher values preserve more variation.
parameter_impact.zero_center: When True, subtracts mean before scaling. Required for PCA which expects centered data. When False, preserves sparsity for sparse matrices.

error_handling.ValueError: X has NaN: Data contains NaN values. Check for zero-variance genes or previous transformations that produced NaN.
error_handling.MemoryError: For large datasets, consider setting max_value to clip extreme values and reduce memory usage.

outliers_dominant.causal_chain: max_value truncates extreme values. Lower values compress the range, reducing the influence of outliers on downstream analysis.
outliers_dominant.expected_effect: Less extreme values, more balanced contribution from all genes

compression_excessive.causal_chain: Higher max_value preserves more of the original variation, allowing highly expressed genes to have stronger influence.
